title: Peppery Gooey Malaysian Fish Pancakes
description: A savory brunchmood fish pancake with rye flour, peppery broth, and a gooey cheese crust, diabeticfriendly with low sugar and high fiber.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together rye flour, oat flour, ground black pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika until well combined.
Add water and almond milk gradually, whisking until a smooth, pourable batter forms.
Stir in the diced tilapia, fish sauce, lime juice, and half of the shredded cheese; let the mixture rest for 5 minutes.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and lightly brush with butter.
Pour a ¼ cup of batter into the skillet, spreading it into a circle about 4 inches in diameter.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese evenly over the pancake and cook until the edges lift, about 2–3 minutes.
Flip gently and cook the other side until golden and the cheese melts, another 2 minutes.
Repeat with the remaining batter, adjusting heat as needed to avoid burning.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired, with a side of low‑sugar lime dipping sauce.
